dependencies: Significant portions of natural gas production dependent on small number of key compression and processing stations
Dependence on third-party pipelines for transportation
Reliance on equipment, rigs, pipe, materials, qualified personnel, water for hydraulic fracturing
Concentration of assets in Appalachian Basin
risks: Supply chain disruptions or labor shortage impacts
Shortages of or delays in obtaining equipment, rigs, pipe, materials, qualified personnel, water
Equipment failures, accidents or unexpected operational events
Aging infrastructure and mechanical or structural problems
Damage to pipelines, wells and storage assets from natural phenomena, weather, sabotage, terrorism
Third-party pipeline unavailability or refusal to accept deliveries
Concentration risk in Appalachian Basin making company vulnerable to regional supply/demand factors, weather, processing/transportation capacity constraints

regulatory_risks: Federal and state environmental regulations including Clean Water Act, Clean Air Act, CERCLA, RCRA
EPA regulation of hydraulic fracturing under Safe Drinking Water Act
FERC regulation of interstate natural gas transportation, storage, and rates
Pipeline safety regulations by PHMSA including integrity management requirements
Climate change and GHG emissions regulations including EPA methane rules
Inflation Reduction Act methane emissions waste charge
State regulations on hydraulic fracturing, water usage, wastewater disposal
Endangered Species Act and Migratory Bird Treaty Act restrictions
Commodity derivatives regulation under Dodd-Frank Act
State and local zoning ordinances and permitting requirements
Potential bans on hydraulic fracturing in certain jurisdictions
Changes to tax laws including potential elimination of oil and gas tax incentives
Regulatory approval delays for new pipeline construction
Potential changes to FERC Certificate Policy Statement
State participation in Regional Greenhouse Gas Initiative (RGGI)
Potential windfall profits tax or LNG export restrictions

sanction_risks: Growing geopolitical instability and armed conflicts (Russia-Ukraine, Middle East) increasing risk of attacks on energy infrastructure
Potential tariffs imposed by United States or other countries on goods and natural resources including natural gas and LNG
Economic sanctions on Russia potentially affecting global energy markets
